title: Amphoricarpos neumayerianus
taxonomy: Amphoricarpos neumayerianus is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It is native to South America. The plant belongs to the genus Amphoricarpos and the order Caryophyllales. Amphoricarpos neumayerianus is a succulent plant that grows in arid regions. Its classification is further specified at the species level as Amphoricarpos neumayerianus.

intro: Amphoricarpos neumayerianus, also known as Neumayer's burr, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the family Boraginaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Amphoricarpos neumayerianus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
